updatedBy: Codex / serialized Phase 0 reconciliation after PRs #266, #270, #271, and #272
lastVerifiedCommit: aca5fb2867ce946afeadfbcb014dfea59c4ae962 after PR #272 merged; GitHub verify, docs, evals, Database contracts, and both Vercel previews passed
maturity: Privacy-safe public POI provenance, reconciled operator-action evidence, the cache-aware LLM cost contract, and four-provider cache-usage normalization are merged. Real Copilot turn/cost/event persistence and the legal/trust routes are independently review-pending, not mainline capabilities. Supavisor compatibility is implemented in a draft PR, but production database authentication must be re-verified after the operator's new DATABASE_URL deployment. The product is not yet information-architecture unified, billing-ready, or production-release complete.
lastCompleted: PR #272 normalized cached-input usage for all four configured providers after PR #271 froze the cache-aware pricing and ledger contract. PR #266 merged privacy-safe public POI provenance, and PR #270 reconciled the operator-action register with observed external configuration. Their reported GitHub checks passed; none of these merges waives outstanding production evidence or phase triggers.

activeWork.state: The operator accepted the dated D3 content baseline. The base-main Tier B/D3 PR is open, clean, non-draft, and its reported GitHub verify, docs, evals, Database contracts, and Vercel checks are green. The routes are not merged and therefore are not public mainline promises.
activeWork.next: Review the public entity, privacy, affiliate, Human Help, and emergency language against the accepted baseline. Merge or request changes; after merge, re-evaluate P0-12 without inventing payment or retention promises.

activeWork.title: Make Postgres.js compatible with Supavisor transaction mode
activeWork.state: The base-main Tier B/D2 draft PR is clean and all reported GitHub checks are green. A sanitized temporary Preview probe previously reached the first database query and returned PostgreSQL 28P01 authentication failure; the probe was fully removed. The operator reports that a fresh DATABASE_URL is now configured in both Vercel projects, but no post-update redeploy/runtime result has been accepted yet.

If authentication passes, record sanitized migration/RLS/runtime evidence, mark the PR ready, and request independent review. If 28P01 persists, keep OA-004 blocked without exposing the URI or password.

blockers: PR #273 / #248c is Tier B review-pending. Its green checks are implementation evidence, not mainline or production-database evidence; 248d and #249 remain serially blocked until it merges.
PR #274 / #75 is D3/Tier B review-pending. P0-12 must not assume the legal/trust routes exist until that PR merges.

OA-004 remains open. The prior fresh Preview failed database authentication with PostgreSQL 28P01. The operator reports a new DATABASE_URL is configured, but a fresh deployment and sanitized runtime smoke are still required before the credential correction is accepted; migration, RLS, durability, backup, and retention evidence follow afterward.
#246 and #247 code are merged, but production Redis behavior still awaits OA-012/OA-013 acceptance. Setting names or local Redis tests do not prove deployed three-turn and trusted-IP behavior.
V2-38 #59 remains D2-blocked until architecture accepts an evidence-eligibility matrix instead of a raw page-count target; downstream SEO Issues #84 through #86 must not generate filler pages.
V2-59 #72 needs architecture to decide the remaining provider-health boundary; V2-61 #74 remains blocked until P0-20 freezes its safety assertions.
Ops RBAC code is merged, but real first-admin and deployed role evidence awaits OA-010.
P0-17 remains blocked by the accepted payment/legal decision gates and deferred OA-006; no real paid service is claimed.
Phase 1/2/3 functional rollout remains trigger-gated even where tooling or schema preparation has merged. No Phase 4 Issue currently exists.

readingOrder.reason: Learn the product thesis, canonical language, relationships, and truth hierarchy.
Understand mission, controlled subsystems, interface baselines, observations, and lifecycle gates.
Understand the composite Qian, Matt-inspired documentation, and Karpathy workflow plus D0-D3 correction model.
See authority, precedence, reading order, document classes, implementation gate, verification, and handoff as one portable baseline.
Read the frozen product, commercial, architecture, roadmap, and anti-goal baseline.
Compare the frozen target with the latest evidence-based implementation audit.
Resolve the one canonical owner, dependencies, trigger gates, and historical migrations before taking any Issue.
Choose the module document that owns the assigned work and inspect its real maturity.
Load the mandatory architecture, coding, business, permission, deployment, and iteration rules.
Apply explicit assumptions, minimum sufficient design, surgical diffs, and step-to-check verification during implementation.
Check accepted decisions before proposing or implementing a direction change.
Read the relevant operational procedure before deployment, migration, AI, knowledge, or Human Task work.
Check unresolved third-party, secret, legal, DNS, deployment, and production-verification actions before claiming an external capability is live.
